revision.text: #redirect[[en:Sōma_Shrine_(Sōma_City)]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

[[福島県]][[相馬市]]にある寺社。
祭神として[[妙見菩薩]]こと[[天之御中主大神]]（あめのみなかぬしのおおかみ）と[[相馬氏]]の氏神である[[平将門]]が[[勧請]]されている。

revision.comment: /* 関連項目 */
revision.text:
'''相馬神社'''（そうまじんじゃ）は[[相馬中村城]]跡に奉られた[[福島県]][[相馬市]]にある[[神社]]。
祭神として[[妙見菩薩]]こと[[天之御中主大神]]（あめのみなかぬしのおおかみ）と[[相馬氏]]の[[氏神]]である[[平将門]]が[[勧請]]されている。
==関連項目== *[[神社一覧]]
{{shinto-stub}} [[Category:福島県の神社|そうましんしや]] 相馬神社は相馬家の始祖師常を祭神として明治１３年（1880）に中村城本丸跡に建てられた神社。 ※馬陵公園（中村城址）には相馬家の氏神としての妙見中村神社と相馬神社の２社がある。妙見中村神社は相馬中村神社ともよばれるため相馬神社と混同されるが別物である。 相馬家は相馬藩領内に居城した原町（妙見太田神社）小高（妙見小高神社）にも当然氏神社を建立しており今も残っており、一般的には中村神社、太田神社、小高神社と表現される。 ※相馬市観光協会、相馬市役所観光データー参照

'''相馬神社'''（そうまじんじゃ）は[[相馬中村城]]跡に奉られた[[福島県]][[相馬市]]にある[[神社]]。
祭神として[[妙見菩薩]]こと[[天之御中主大神]]（あめのみなかぬしのおおかみ）と[[相馬氏]]の[[氏神]]である[[平将門]]が[[勧請]]されている。 ==概要== 相馬家の始祖[[相馬師常|師常]]を祭神として[[1880年|明治13年（1880年）]]に建立された。[[馬陵公園]]（中村城址）には相馬家の氏神としての'''妙見中村神社'''と'''相馬神社'''の2社がある。妙見中村神社は相馬中村神社ともよばれるため相馬神社と混同されるが別物である。相馬家は[[相馬藩]]領内に居城した[[原町市|原町]]、[[小高町|小高]]にもそれぞれ妙見太田神社と妙見小高神社を建立したものが現在も残っており、一般的には'''中村神社'''、'''太田神社'''、'''小高神社'''と表現される。 ==関連項目== *[[神社一覧]] *[[相馬野馬追]] *[[川嶋舟]] - [[文仁親王妃紀子]]の実弟。現在の神主の娘と結婚。 {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:福島県の神社|そうましんしや]]

'''相馬神社'''（そうまじんじゃ）は[[相馬中村城]]跡に奉られた[[福島県]][[相馬市]]にある[[神社]]。
祭神として[[妙見菩薩]]こと[[天之御中主大神]]（あめのみなかぬしのおおかみ）と[[相馬氏]]の[[氏神]]である[[平将門]]が[[勧請]]されている。 ==概要== 相馬家の始祖[[相馬師常|師常]]を祭神として[[1880年|明治13年（1880年）]]に建立された。[[馬陵公園]]（中村城址）には相馬家の氏神としての'''妙見中村神社'''と'''相馬神社'''の2社がある。妙見中村神社は相馬中村神社ともよばれるため相馬神社と混同されるが別物である。相馬家は[[相馬藩]]領内に居城した[[原町市|原町]]、[[小高町|小高]]にもそれぞれ妙見太田神社と妙見小高神社を建立したものが現在も残っており、一般的には'''中村神社'''、'''太田神社'''、'''小高神社'''と表現される。 ==関連項目== *[[神社一覧]] *[[相馬野馬追]] *[[川嶋舟]] - [[文仁親王妃紀子]]の実弟。現在の神主の娘と結婚。 {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:福島県の神社|そうましんしや]] [http://www.somanakamurajinjya.or.jp/ 相馬中村神社ホームページ]

revision.comment: 相馬神社本殿画像追加

revision.text:
{{右|[[画像:Soma shrine 20070413121928.jpg|thumb|none|250px|相馬神社([[2007年]][[4月13日]])]]}}
'''相馬神社'''（そうまじんじゃ）は[[相馬中村城]]跡に奉られた[[福島県]][[相馬市]]にある[[神社]]。
祭神として[[妙見菩薩]]こと[[天之御中主大神]]（あめのみなかぬしのおおかみ）と[[相馬氏]]の[[氏神]]である[[平将門]]が[[勧請]]されている。 ==概要== 相馬家の始祖[[相馬師常|師常]]を祭神として[[1880年|明治13年（1880年）]]に建立された。[[馬陵公園]]（中村城址）には相馬家の氏神としての'''妙見中村神社'''と'''相馬神社'''の2社がある。妙見中村神社は相馬中村神社ともよばれるため相馬神社と混同されるが別物である。相馬家は[[相馬藩]]領内に居城した[[原町市|原町]]、[[小高町|小高]]にもそれぞれ妙見太田神社と妙見小高神社を建立したものが現在も残っており、一般的には'''中村神社'''、'''太田神社'''、'''小高神社'''と表現される。
== 関連項目 == *[[神社一覧]] *[[相馬野馬追]] *[[川嶋舟]] - [[文仁親王妃紀子]]の実弟。現在の神主の娘と結婚。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.somanakamurajinjya.or.jp/ 相馬中村神社ホームページ]
{{shinto-stub}} [[Category:福島県の神社|そうましんしや]]

拝殿([[2011年]][[1月7日]])}} '''相馬神社'''（そうまじんじゃ）は[[相馬中村城]]跡に奉られた[[福島県]][[相馬市]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は県社。 祭神として[[妙見菩薩]]こと[[天之御中主大神]]（あめのみなかぬしのおおかみ）と[[相馬氏]]の[[氏神]]である[[平将門]]が[[勧請]]されている。 ==概要== 相馬家の始祖[[相馬師常|師常]]を祭神として[[1880年|明治13年（1880年）]]に建立された。[[馬陵公園]]（中村城址）には相馬家の氏神としての'''妙見中村神社'''と'''相馬神社'''の2社がある。妙見中村神社は[[相馬中村神社]]ともよばれるため相馬神社と混同されるが別物である。相馬家は[[相馬藩]]領内に居城した[[原町市|原町]]、[[小高町|小高]]にもそれぞれ妙見太田神社と妙見小高神社を建立したものが現在も残っており、一般的には'''中村神社'''、'''太田神社'''、'''小高神社'''と表現される。 == 関連項目 == *[[神社一覧]] *[[相馬野馬追]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.somanakamurajinjya.or.jp/ 相馬中村神社ホームページ] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:そうましんしや}} [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:相馬市]] [[Category:福島県の旧県社]]

{{神社| 名称=相馬神社| 所在地= 福島県相馬市中村字北町101| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000}}| 祭神=相馬師常、天之御中主大神、平将門| 創建=明治13年（1880年）| 社格=県社| 例祭=御神楽大会（10月第1日曜日）| 本殿=| 画像=[[File:Soma Shrine.JPG|250px]]
拝殿([[2011年]][[1月7日]])}} '''相馬神社'''（そうまじんじゃ）は[[相馬中村城]]跡に奉られた[[福島県]][[相馬市]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は県社。 祭神として[[妙見菩薩]]こと[[天之御中主大神]]（あめのみなかぬしのおおかみ）と[[相馬氏]]の[[氏神]]である[[平将門]]が[[勧請]]されている。 ==概要== 相馬家の始祖[[相馬師常|師常]]を祭神として[[1880年|明治13年（1880年）]]に建立された。[[馬陵公園]]（中村城址）には相馬家の氏神としての'''妙見中村神社'''と'''相馬神社'''の2社がある。妙見中村神社は[[相馬中村神社]]ともよばれるため相馬神社と混同されるが別物である。相馬家は[[相馬藩]]領内に居城した[[原町市|原町]]、[[小高町|小高]]にもそれぞれ妙見太田神社と妙見小高神社を建立したものが現在も残っており、一般的には'''中村神社'''、'''太田神社'''、'''小高神社'''と表現される。 == 関連項目 == *[[神社一覧]] *[[相馬野馬追]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.somanakamurajinjya.or.jp/ 相馬中村神社ホームページ] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:そうましんしや}} [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧県社]] [[Category:相馬市の歴史]]

[[相馬中村城]]内・相馬神社([[2013年]][[6月3日]])}} [[File:Soma Shrine.JPG|thumb|拝殿(2011年1月7日）]] [[File:Soma shrine 001.JPG|thumb|相馬神社入口(2013年6月3日）]] '''相馬神社'''（そうまじんじゃ）は[[相馬中村城]]跡に奉られた[[福島県]][[相馬市]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は県社。[[江戸時代]]後期から[[明治時代]]初期に流行した[[藩祖]]を祀った神社のひとつ。 祭神として[[妙見菩薩]]こと[[天之御中主大神]]（あめのみなかぬしのおおかみ）と[[相馬氏]]の[[氏神]]である[[平将門]]が[[勧請]]されている。 ==概要== 相馬家の始祖[[相馬師常|師常]]を祭神として[[1880年|明治13年（1880年）]]に建立された。[[馬陵公園]]（中村城址）には相馬家の氏神としての'''妙見中村神社'''と'''相馬神社'''の2社がある。妙見中村神社は[[相馬中村神社]]ともよばれるため相馬神社と混同されるが別物である。相馬家は[[相馬中村藩]]領内に居城した[[原町市|原町]]、[[小高町|小高]]にもそれぞれ妙見太田神社と妙見小高神社を建立したものが現在も残っており、一般的には'''中村神社'''、'''太田神社'''、'''小高神社'''と表現される。 == 関連項目 == *[[神社一覧]] *[[相馬野馬追]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.somanakamurajinjya.or.jp/ 相馬中村神社ホームページ] {{DEFAULTSORT:そうましんしや}} [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧県社]] [[Category:相馬神社]] [[Category:相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:相馬氏|社]] [[Category:平将門]] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref}}

[[相馬中村城]]内・相馬神社([[2013年]][[6月3日]])|地図={{Location map|Japan Fukushima|lat_deg=37|lat_min=47|lat_sec=53.7|lon_deg=140|lon_min=54|lon_sec=52.2|label=相馬神社|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}} '''相馬神社'''（そうまじんじゃ）は[[相馬中村城]]跡に奉られた[[福島県]][[相馬市]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は県社。[[江戸時代]]後期から[[明治時代]]初期に流行した[[藩祖]]を祀った神社のひとつ。 祭神として[[妙見菩薩]]こと[[天之御中主大神]]（あめのみなかぬしのおおかみ）と[[相馬氏]]の[[氏神]]である[[平将門]]が[[勧請]]されている。 ==概要== 相馬家の始祖[[相馬師常|師常]]を祭神として[[1880年|明治13年（1880年）]]に建立された。[[馬陵公園]]（中村城址）には相馬家の氏神としての'''妙見中村神社'''と'''相馬神社'''の2社がある。妙見中村神社は[[相馬中村神社]]ともよばれるため相馬神社と混同されるが別物である。相馬家は[[相馬中村藩]]領内に居城した[[原町市|原町]]、[[小高町|小高]]にもそれぞれ妙見太田神社と妙見小高神社を建立したものが現在も残っており、一般的には'''中村神社'''、'''太田神社'''、'''小高神社'''と表現される。 File:Soma shrine 001.JPG|thumb|相馬神社入口（2013年6月3日） File:Soma Shrine.JPG|thumb|拝殿（2011年1月7日） == 関連項目 == *[[神社一覧]] *[[相馬野馬追]] *[[相馬氏]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.somanakamurajinjya.or.jp/ 相馬中村神社ホームページ] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=福島県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:そうましんしや}} [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧県社]] [[Category:相馬神社]] [[Category:相馬市の建築物]] [[Category:相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:相馬氏|社]] [[Category:平将門]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Sōma Shrine | image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Sōma Shrine | image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Sōma Shrine | image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Sōma Shrine | image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Sōma Shrine | image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Sōma Shrine | image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} revision.comment: adding religious affiliation to the ones with a broken religious affiliation section
revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} {{Shrine | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代type制度}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代type制度}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代type制度}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. {{Shrine | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代type制度}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. {{Shrine | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代type制度}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代type制度}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} {{Shrine | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代type制度}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. {{Shrine | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代type制度}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代type制度}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代type制度}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代type制度}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代type制度}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代type制度}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代type制度}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代type制度}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代type制度}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} [[Category:Unsorted {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}
