{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} [[Category:Unsorted revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} [[Category:Unsorted revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[en:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} [[Category:Unsorted revision.comment: removed [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]]; added [[Category:Pages to be translated]] using [[Help:Gadget-HotCat|HotCat]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} revision.comment: Text replacement - "|平将門}}" to "|平将門|1=Taira no Masakado}}"
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門|1=Taira no Masakado}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門|1=Taira no Masakado}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} [[Category:Completion tier revision.comment: Text replacement - "平将門" to "Taira no Masakado"
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} [[Category:Completion tier revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} [[Category:Completion tier revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 1]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} [[Category:Completion tier revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} [[Category:Completion tier revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} [[Category:Completion tier revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} [[Category:Completion tier {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} [[Category:Completion tier revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} [[Category:Completion tier {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} [[Category:Completion tier revision.comment: Adding [[Category:return the see also]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} [[Category:Completion tier revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == See Also! == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}}
