revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also! ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} [[Category:Completion tier revision.comment: Text replacement - "3年" to "3rd year "
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13rd year }} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13rd year }} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} [[Category:Completion tier {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13rd year }} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13rd year }} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Tier 1]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13rd year }} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13rd year }} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)]][[Category:11]] revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13rd year }} | shrine rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13rd year }} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神|12=draft|12=draft}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=draft|12=draft}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13rd year|12=draft|12=draft}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常|12=draft|12=draft}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園|12=draft|12=draft}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市|12=draft|12=draft}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Sōma Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sōma City]] [[Category:History of Sōma City]] [[Category:Sōma clan]] [[Category:Taira no Masakado]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)]] [[ja:相馬神社_(相馬市)]] [[en:Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)}}

Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. revision.comment: 40 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:相馬神社 (相馬市) up to rev 95960878
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2023年7月}} {{神社 | 名称 = 相馬神社 | 画像 = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
[[相馬中村城]]内・相馬神社([[2013年]][[6月3日]]) | 所在地 = [[福島県]][[相馬市]]中村字北町101 | 位置 = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=相馬神社}} | 祭神 = {{Ublist| [[相馬師常]] | [[天之御中主大神]] | [[平将門]] }} | 創建 = [[明治]]13年（[[1880年]]） | 社格 = 県社 | 例祭 = 御神楽大会（10月第1日曜日） | 本殿 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=相馬神社|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''相馬神社'''（そうまじんじゃ）は[[相馬中村城]]跡に奉られた[[福島県]][[相馬市]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は県社。[[江戸時代]]後期から[[明治時代]]初期に流行した[[藩祖]]を祀った神社のひとつ。 祭神として[[妙見菩薩]]こと[[天之御中主大神]]（あめのみなかぬしのおおかみ）と[[相馬氏]]の[[氏神]]である[[平将門]]が[[勧請]]されている。 ==概要== 相馬家の始祖[[相馬師常|師常]]を祭神として[[明治]]13年（[[1880年]]）に建立された。[[馬陵公園]]（中村城址）には相馬家の氏神としての'''妙見中村神社'''と'''相馬神社'''の2社がある。妙見中村神社は[[相馬中村神社]]ともよばれるため相馬神社と混同されるが別物である。相馬家は[[相馬中村藩]]領内に居城した[[原町区|原町]]、[[小高区|小高]]（いずれも[[南相馬市]]）にもそれぞれ妙見太田神社と妙見小高神社を建立したものが現在も残っており、一般的には'''中村神社'''、'''太田神社'''、'''小高神社'''と表現される。 Soma shrine 001.JPG|相馬神社入口（2013年6月3日） Soma Shrine.JPG|拝殿（2011年1月7日） == 関連項目 == * [[神社一覧]] * [[相馬野馬追]] * [[相馬氏]] == 外部リンク == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ 相馬神社] - 相馬市観光協会 {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=福島県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:そうましんしや}} [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧県社]] [[Category:相馬神社]] [[Category:相馬市の建築物]] [[Category:相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:相馬氏|社]] [[Category:平将門]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg|250px]]
Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sōma City|ja|相馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | shrine Rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|相馬中村城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Under the {{ill|modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma City|ja|南相馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|ja|神社一覧|1=List of Shinto shrines in Japan|2=ja|3=神社一覧|4=zh|5=日本神社列表|6=uk|7=Список синтоїстських святилищ|8=es|9=Anexo:Santuarios sintoístas|10=fr|11=Liste de sanctuaires shinto japonais|12=it|13=Lista di santuari shintoisti|14=simple|15=List of Shinto shrines|16=mr|17=जपानमधील शिंतो तीर्थस्थानांची यादी|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Sōma Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sōma City]] [[Category:History of Sōma City]] [[Category:Sōma clan]] [[Category:Taira no Masakado]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)]] [[ja:相馬神社_(相馬市)]] [[en:Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)}} {{translated page|ja|相馬神社 (相馬市)|version=95960878|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It is built on the site of the former [[:en:Sōma Nakamura Castle|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}}, identified with [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]], and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=}}, the tutelary deity of the [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]]. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1880|qq=}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritsune|qq=}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baryō Park|qq=}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nakamura Shrine|qq=}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Ōta Shrine|qq=}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Odaka Shrine|qq=}} in Odaka, both located in what is now [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma City]]. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Sōma, Fukushima|Sōma City]], Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritsune|qq=}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}}} | founded = {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1880|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1880年}} | shrine Rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former [[:en:Sōma Nakamura Castle|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, identified with [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]], and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the tutelary deity of the [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]]. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|ja|明治13年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1880|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1880年}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritsune|qq=}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baryō Park|qq=}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nakamura Shrine|qq=}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Ōta Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Odaka Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Odaka, both located in what is now [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma City]]. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Sōma Shrine within the Nakamura Castle grounds (June 3, 2013) | address = [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Sōma, Fukushima|Sōma City]], Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37|47|53.7|N|140|54|52.2|E|scale:20000_region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Shrine}} | deity = {{Ublist|{{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritsune|qq=}}|{{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}|{{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}}} | founded = [[:en:1880|1880]] | shrine Rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former [[:en:Sōma Nakamura Castle|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, identified with [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]], and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the tutelary deity of the [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]]. == Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in [[:en:1880|1880]] to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritsune|qq=}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baryō Park|qq=}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nakamura Shrine|qq=}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Ōta Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Odaka Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Odaka, both located in what is now [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma City]]. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine. Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) == See Also == * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Japan|List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} * [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]] == External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Fukushima Prefecture}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|相馬神社 (相馬市)|version=95960878|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Sōma clan]] [[Category:Taira no Masakado]] }} [[sn:Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)]] [[ja:相馬神社_(相馬市)]] [[Category:Taira no Masakado]] [[Category:相馬市の建築物]] [[Category:相馬の Municipal History]] [[Category:相馬神社]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Soma Shrine (Fukushima)}} [[ja:相馬神社 (相馬市)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Soma Shrine (Fukushima)]][[ja:相馬神社 (相馬市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11582527]] Wikidata name: Sōma Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg | address = [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Sōma, Fukushima|Sōma City]], Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37.79825|N|140.9145|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Q11582495|qid=Q11582495}} | founded = [[:en:1880|1880]] | shrine Rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | location = {{ill|Sōma|qid=Q696456}} }}
'''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former [[:en:Sōma Nakamura Castle|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders.
The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, identified with [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]], and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the tutelary deity of the [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]].
== Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in [[:en:1880|1880]] to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritsune|qq=|1=Sōma Moritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582495}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baryō Park|qq=|1=Baryō Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97183040}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle.
The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nakamura Shrine|qq=}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Ōta Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Odaka Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Odaka, both located in what is now [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma City]]. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine.

'''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former [[:en:Sōma Nakamura Castle|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders.
The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, identified with [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]], and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the tutelary deity of the [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]].
== Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in [[:en:1880|1880]] to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritsune|qq=|1=Sōma Moritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582495}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baryō Park|qq=|1=Baryō Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97183040}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle.
The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nakamura Shrine|qq=}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Ōta Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Odaka Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Odaka, both located in what is now [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma City]]. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine.
<gallery> Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) </gallery> == See Also == * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Japan|List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} * [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]]
== External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association]

'''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former [[:en:Sōma Nakamura Castle|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders.
The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, identified with [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]], and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the tutelary deity of the [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]].
== Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in [[:en:1880|1880]] to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritsune|1=Sōma Moritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582495}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baryō Park|1=Baryō Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97183040}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle.
The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nakamura Shrine|qq=}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Odaka, both located in what is now [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma City]]. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Soma Shrine (Fukushima)]][[ja:相馬神社 (相馬市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11582527]] Wikidata name: Sōma Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg | address = [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Sōma, Fukushima|Sōma City]], Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37.79825|N|140.9145|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Q11582495|WD=Q11582495}} | founded = [[:en:1880|1880]] | shrine Rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | location = {{ill|Sōma|WD=Q696456}} }}
'''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former [[:en:Sōma Nakamura Castle|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders.
The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, identified with [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]], and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the tutelary deity of the [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]].
== Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in [[:en:1880|1880]] to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritsune|1=Sōma Moritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582495}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baryō Park|1=Baryō Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97183040}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle.
The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nakamura Shrine|qq=}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Odaka, both located in what is now [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma City]]. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine.
<gallery> Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) </gallery> == See Also == * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Japan|List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} * [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Soma Shrine (Fukushima)]][[ja:相馬神社 (相馬市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11582527]] Wikidata name: Sōma Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg | address = [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Sōma, Fukushima|Sōma City]], Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37.79825|N|140.9145|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Q11582495|WD=Q11582495}} | founded = [[:en:1880|1880]] | shrine Rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | location = {{ill|Sōma|WD=Q696456}} }}
'''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former [[:en:Sōma Nakamura Castle|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders.
The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, identified with [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]], and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the tutelary deity of the [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]].
== Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in [[:en:1880|1880]] to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritsune|1=Sōma Moritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582495}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baryō Park|1=Baryō Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97183040}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle.
The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nakamura Shrine|qq=}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Odaka, both located in what is now [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma City]]. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine.
<gallery> Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) </gallery> == See Also == * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Japan|List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} * [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]]
== External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association]

'''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former [[:en:Sōma Nakamura Castle|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders.
The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, identified with [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]], and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the tutelary deity of the [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]].
== Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in [[:en:1880|1880]] to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritsune|1=Sōma Moritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582495}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baryō Park|1=Baryō Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97183040}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle.
The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nakamura Shrine|qq=}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Odaka, both located in what is now [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma City]]. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine.
<gallery> Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) </gallery> == See Also == * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Japan|List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} * [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Soma Shrine (Fukushima)]][[ja:相馬神社 (相馬市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Soma Shrine (Fukushima)]][[ja:相馬神社 (相馬市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Soma Shrine (Fukushima)]][[ja:相馬神社 (相馬市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11582527]] Wikidata name: Sōma Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg | address = [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Sōma, Fukushima|Sōma City]], Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37.79825|N|140.9145|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Q11582495|WD=Q11582495}} | founded = [[:en:1880|1880]] | shrine Rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | location = {{ill|Sōma|WD=Q696456}} }}
'''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former [[:en:Sōma Nakamura Castle|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders.
The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, identified with [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]], and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the tutelary deity of the [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]].
== Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in [[:en:1880|1880]] to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritsune|1=Sōma Moritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582495}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baryō Park|1=Baryō Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97183040}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle.
The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nakamura Shrine|qq=}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Odaka, both located in what is now [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma City]]. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine.
<gallery> Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) </gallery> == See Also == * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Japan|List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} * [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]]
== External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Soma Shrine (Fukushima)]][[ja:相馬神社 (相馬市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Soma Shrine (Fukushima)]][[ja:相馬神社 (相馬市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Soma Shrine (Fukushima)]][[ja:相馬神社 (相馬市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11582527]] Wikidata name: Sōma Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sōma Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Soma Shrine (Fukushima).jpg | address = [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Sōma, Fukushima|Sōma City]], Nakamura Aza Kitamachi 101 | coordinates = {{Coord|37.79825|N|140.9145|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Q11582495|WD=Q11582495}} | founded = [[:en:1880|1880]] | shrine Rank = Prefectural Shrine (県社) | festival = Kagura Festival (First Sunday of October) | main hall = | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | location = {{ill|Sōma|WD=Q696456}} }}
'''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former [[:en:Sōma Nakamura Castle|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders.
The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, identified with [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]], and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the tutelary deity of the [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]].
== Overview == Sōma Shrine was established in [[:en:1880|1880]] to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritsune|1=Sōma Moritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582495}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baryō Park|1=Baryō Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97183040}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle.
The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nakamura Shrine|qq=}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Odaka, both located in what is now [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma City]]. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine.
<gallery> Soma shrine 001.JPG|Entrance to Sōma Shrine (2013) Soma Shrine.JPG|Worship Hall (2011) </gallery> == See Also == * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines in Japan|List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} * [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]]
== External Links == * [https://soma-kanko.jp/trip/soma-zinzya/ Sōma Shrine - Sōma City Tourism Association]

Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baryō Park|1=Baryō Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97183040}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle.
The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nakamura Shrine|qq=}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Odaka, both located in what is now [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma City]]. '''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Castle|en|Sōma Nakamura Castle|WD=Q11582470|lt=Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)}}. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders.

The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|WD=Q10960623|lt=Myōken Bosatsu}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|en|Sōma clan|WD=Q2975379|lt=Sōma clan}}.

== Overview ==
Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|en|1880|WD=Q7806|lt=1880}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritsune|1=Sōma Moritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582495}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baryō Park|1=Baryō Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97183040}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. 

The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nakamura Shrine|qq=}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma|en|Minamisōma|WD=Q642094|lt=Minamisōma City}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine.

'''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Castle|en|Sōma Nakamura Castle|WD=Q11582470|lt=Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)}}. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders.

The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|WD=Q10960623|lt=Myōken Bosatsu}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|en|Sōma clan|WD=Q2975379|lt=Sōma clan}}.

== Overview ==
Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|en|1880|WD=Q7806|lt=1880}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritsune|1=Sōma Moritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582495}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baryō Park|1=Baryō Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97183040}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. 

The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nakamura Shrine|qq=}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma|en|Minamisōma|WD=Q642094|lt=Minamisōma City}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine.

'''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Castle|en|Sōma Nakamura Castle|WD=Q11582470|lt=Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)}}. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders.

The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|WD=Q10960623|lt=Myōken Bosatsu}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|en|Sōma clan|WD=Q2975379|lt=Sōma clan}}.

== Overview ==
Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|en|1880|WD=Q7806|lt=1880}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritsune|1=Sōma Moritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582495}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baryō Park|1=Baryō Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97183040}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. 

The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nakamura Shrine|qq=}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma|en|Minamisōma|WD=Q642094|lt=Minamisōma City}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine.

'''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Castle|en|Sōma Nakamura Castle|WD=Q11582470|lt=Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|1=Sōma Nakamura Castle}}. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders.

The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|WD=Q10960623|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|1=Myōken}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|en|Sōma clan|WD=Q2975379|lt=Sōma clan|1=Sōma clan}}.

== Overview ==
Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|en|1880|WD=Q7806|lt=1880|1=1880}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritsune|1=Q11582495|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582495}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baryō Park|1=Baryō Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97183040}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. 

The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nakamura Shrine|qq=}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma|en|Minamisōma|WD=Q642094|lt=Minamisōma City|1=Minamisōma}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine.

'''Sōma Shrine''' (相馬神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is built on the site of the former {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Castle|en|Sōma Nakamura Castle|WD=Q11582470|lt=Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|1=Sōma Nakamura Castle}}. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a prefectural shrine (県社). The shrine is one of several established during the late Edo and early Meiji periods to honor domain founders.

The enshrined deities are {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Kami|ja|天之御中主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, identified with {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|WD=Q10960623|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|1=Myōken}}, and {{ill|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Sōma clan|en|Sōma clan|WD=Q2975379|lt=Sōma clan|1=Sōma clan}}.

== Overview ==
Sōma Shrine was established in {{ill|1880|en|1880|WD=Q7806|lt=1880|1=1880}} to enshrine {{ill|Sōma Moritsune|ja|相馬師常|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritsune|1=Q11582495|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582495}}, the founder of the Sōma clan. The shrine is located within {{ill|Baryō Park|ja|馬陵公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baryō Park|1=Baryō Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97183040}}, which encompasses the ruins of Nakamura Castle. 

The park also contains the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Shrine|ja|相馬中村神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nakamura Shrine|qq=}}, which is sometimes referred to as Myōken Nakamura Shrine. Despite their proximity, Sōma Shrine and Sōma Nakamura Shrine are separate entities. Additionally, other shrines related to the Sōma clan include {{ill|Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja|妙見太田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Ōta Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Haramachi and {{ill|Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja|妙見小高神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Odaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Odaka, both located in what is now {{ill|Minamisōma|en|Minamisōma|WD=Q642094|lt=Minamisōma City|1=Minamisōma}}. Collectively, these shrines are commonly referred to as Nakamura Shrine, Ōta Shrine, and Odaka Shrine.
